*** 4 FATAL *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 1 ENE EMMALENA. 4 CHILDREN, RANGING FROM 1 TO 8 YEARS OLD, PERISHED IN THE FLOOD WATERS OF MONTGOMERY AND TROUBLESOME CREEKS AFTER BEING SWEPT AWAY FROM THEIR PARENTS. NUMEROUS NEWS ARTICLES STATE THAT THE FAMILY WAS AWOKEN BY THE FLASH FLOOD WARNING AROUND 2:00 AM EDT. (THE CONSIDERABLE TAG THAT SET OFF THE WEA ALERTS WAS SENT OUT AT 1:38 AM - SO THIS IS LIKELY THE TIME AROUND WHEN THE PARENTS WERE ACTUALLY AWAKENED.) THE REPORTS GO ON TO STATE THAT IT WAS A MATTER OF MINUTES BEFORE THE FAMILY WAS FORCED TO ESCAPE TO THE ROOF OF THEIR TRAILER ONCE WATER STARTED POURING IN. THEY WERE NOT ON THE ROOF VERY LONG BEFORE THEY REALIZED THE TRAILER WAS NOT STABLE, AND THEY SWAM TO A NEARBY TREE. IT WAS OVER THE COURSE OF SOME TIME THAT THE CHILDREN GOT RIPPED AWAY FROM THE PARENTS GRASP, AND AROUND 8 HOURS BEFORE THE PARENTS WERE RESCUED. 2 OF THE CHILDREN WERE FOUND DECEASED ON THURSDAY, THE OTHER TWO ON FRIDAY MORNING.

AN OLDER BARN LOST A LARGE SECTION OF THE ROOFING, CAUSING ONE SIDE TO COLLAPSE. THE ROOF SECTIONS WERE INTACT AND TRAVELED ABOUT 30 FEET EASTWARD, AWAY FROM THE BARN. A LARGE TREE BRANCH WAS ALSO SNAPPED IN THE SAME LOCATION. THE NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM ASSESSED WINDS AROUND 61 MPH IN THIS LOCATION.

SEVERAL LOCUST TREES WERE SNAPPED, BUT THE NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM NOTED THAT MOST OF THEM HAD ROTTEN TRUNKS. ALL TREES WERE UNIFORMLY LAYING TO THE EAST. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 61 MPH IN THIS LOCATION.

MULTIPLE BARNS AND FARM STRUCTURES (CORN CRIB BIN AND CHICKEN COOP) WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. NWS STORM SURVEY RESULTS NOTED THAT THE SMALL BUILDINGS WERE LIGHT AND NOT WELL ANCHORED DOWN, THAT THE METAL POLE BARN WAS OPEN ON THE ENDS, AND THAT THE STICK BUILT BARN WAS OLD AND ALSO HAD A DOOR OPEN TO ALLOW WINDS TO GET INSIDE. ALL DAMAGE WAS LAYING UNIFORMLY TOWARDS THE ENE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 61 MPH.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS LED TO THE DESTRUCTION OF 2 BARNS ON A PROPERTY. THE BARNS WERE OLD, AND WERE NOT ANCHORED DOWN TO THE GROUND. ONE BARN WAS MOVED OFF ITS FOUNDATION BY ABOUT 15 FEET AND COLLAPSED. THE OTHER LOST ROOFING MATERIAL AND HAD MUTLPLE NORTH FACING WALL PANELS BLOWN OFF. NWS SURVEY TEAM ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 61 MPH.

A PARTIAL BARN COLLAPSE. THE WALLS OF THE BARN DID LOOK TO BE ANCHORED WITH A PIECE OF REBARB STICKING OUT OF THE CONCRETE FOOTERS, AND INTO A HOLE AT THE BOTTOM OF THE 6X6 INCH BARN POSTS/VERTICAL BEAMS. NO OTHER ANCHORING CLIPS WERE USED, SO WIND WAS ABLE TO LIFT POST OFF OF ANCHOR. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM ALSO NOTED THAT MOST OF THE WOOD WAS DRY ROTTED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 65 MPH.

A PEG CONSTRUCTION BARN COLLAPSED WITH MOST DEBRIS UNIFORMLY LAYING TOWARDS THE ENE AND NE. THE ROOF WAS DISLODGED AND TOSSED 150 FEET TO THE EAST OF THE BARN, BUT THE FRAMING OF THE ROOF SEEMED TO BE ROTTED. THE CEMENT FOOTERS THAT THE BARN WAS SITTING ON SHOWED NO INDICATION OF BEING ANCHORED TO THE BARN - RATHER THE WEIGHT OF THE BARN WAS HOLDING IT UP. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 61 MPH.
A ROTTEN OAK TREE WAS SNAPPED. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 61 MPH.
